questions.question: Explain the concept of encapsulation in Python with the help of a practical example. Discuss how data hiding is achieved in Python using name mangling (double underscore prefix) and the use of property decorators (@property) to implement getters and setters. Also, differentiate between public, protected, and private access specifiers in Python with code examples.
keyPoints: Definition of encapsulation as bundling data and methods
Data hiding through underscore conventions (_protected, __private/name mangling)
@property decorator for controlled access
Getter and setter methods vs property decorators
Difference between public, protected, and private attributes in Python

questions.question: Design a class hierarchy for a university management system with a base class 'Person' and derived classes 'Student' and 'Professor'. Implement the following: (a) Demonstrate polymorphism by creating a method 'display_details()' that behaves differently in each subclass. (b) Show method overriding by implementing 'calculate_grade()' in Student class. (c) Use super() to invoke parent class constructor. (d) Implement an abstract method concept using ABC module.
keyPoints: Inheritance: single inheritance from Person to Student and Professor
Polymorphism through method overriding
super() function for parent constructor invocation
ABC module for abstract base class and abstract methods
Method overriding with different implementations

questions.question: Differentiate between instance methods, class methods, and static methods in Python with appropriate code examples. Explain when and why you would use each type. Also, demonstrate how @classmethod and @staticmethod decorators are used, and explain the significance of 'self' and 'cls' parameters.
keyPoints: Instance methods: first parameter is 'self', operate on instance variables
Class methods: first parameter is 'cls', operate on class variables, use @classmethod
Static methods: no automatic parameter, behave like regular functions, use @staticmethod
Use cases: class methods for factory patterns, static methods for utility functions
Differences in method calling: obj.method() vs Class.method()

questions.question: Create a Python program to model a 'BankAccount' class with the following requirements: (a) Implement encapsulation to protect the account balance using private attributes. (b) Include methods for deposit() and withdraw() with proper validation (e.g., insufficient funds check). (c) Implement operator overloading using __str__, __repr__, and __add__ methods. (d) Create a class variable to track total number of accounts and a class method to display it. (e) Demonstrate the creation of objects and accessing of all features.
keyPoints: Private attributes using double underscore (__balance)
Validation in deposit and withdraw methods
__str__ for user-friendly output, __repr__ for developer representation
Operator overloading (__add__ for combining balances)
Class variables for tracking total accounts
Class method to access class-level data

questions.question: Explain the role of __init__ (constructor) and __del__ (destructor) methods in Python classes. Discuss how they are automatically invoked and their practical applications. Also, explain the significance of __str__ and __repr__ magic methods, highlighting the difference between them with suitable examples.
keyPoints: __init__: constructor called when object is created, initialize attributes
__del__: destructor called when object is garbage collected
Automatic invocation of magic methods
__str__: readable string representation for end users
__repr__: unambiguous representation for developers
When to implement each and practical examples
